name: phoenixd
description: ACINQ's phoenixd — a headless Lightning Network node with automatic liquidity. Exposes an HTTP API to send/receive Lightning payments.

inputs.label: API password
inputs.help: Full-access HTTP API password (Basic auth, blank username). Stored encrypted.

inputs.label: Auto-liquidity
inputs.help: Inbound liquidity automatically requested from the LSP (sat). Off disables auto-liquidity.

compose:
name: "slashnode-phoenixd"
services:
  phoenixd:
    image: "acinq/phoenixd:0.8.0"
    container_name: "phoenixd"
    restart: "unless-stopped"
    networks:
      - slashnode
    command:
      - "--http-password=${secret.HTTP_PASSWORD}"
      - "--chain=${input.NETWORK}"
      - "--auto-liquidity=${input.AUTO_LIQUIDITY}"
    ports:
      - "127.0.0.1:9740:9740"
    volumes:
      - "phoenixd-data:/phoenix/.phoenix"
networks:
  slashnode:
    external: true
volumes:
  phoenixd-data:
    name: "slashnode-phoenixd_phoenixd_phoenixd-data"
notes: ⚠️ This API controls your funds and must NOT be exposed to the open internet. It is published only on 127.0.0.1:9740 and on the slashnode network — reach it remotely via the app's .onion (Tor + Basic auth) or an SSH tunnel. Authenticate with HTTP Basic auth: blank username, the API password as the password (curl -u :PASSWORD http://phoenixd:9740/getinfo). On first start phoenixd generates a wallet seed at /phoenix/.phoenix/seed.dat — back it up. Webhooks can be added in /phoenix/.phoenix/phoenix.conf.
